original_hypothesis.title: Admin Rug Pull via Unrestricted Withdraw
original_hypothesis.hypothesis_description: The owner can call the `withdraw` function at any time to extract all TITN tokens from the contract, instantly rendering the protocol insolvent and stealing all user funds.
original_hypothesis.investigation_rationale: While admin privileges are common, the ability to withdraw the exact tokens allocated to users (TITN) without any timelock or conditional checks violates the core economic invariant of the protocol.

proof_of_signal.pattern: token.safeTransfer(owner(), amount) with no restrictions on token type or amount

preconditions: Admin is malicious or private key is compromised

initial_economic_evidence.suspicious_calculation: Unrestricted transfer of any ERC20 balance to owner
initial_economic_evidence.profit_hypothesis: Admin steals 100% of the protocol's TVL

economic_questions: Is there any off-chain multi-sig or timelock protecting the owner role?
validation_requirements: Verify that withdraw() can drain the TITN token specifically

validation_result.impact_summary: Filtered before validation: attack_realism is LOW
validation_result.detailed_analysis: This hypothesis was auto-rejected because its attack_realism grade is LOW. Preconditions require actions outside the attacker's control (off-chain compromise, victim cooperation, or admin collusion). No LLM validation was performed.
